mode_switching_guide.overview: This mode focuses on packaging and deployment. When packaging reveals widget implementation issues, explicit handoff to the widget authoring mode is required.

scenario.trigger: Packaging fails due to missing or invalid widget files
scenario.reason: Widget structure problems require widget implementation expertise, not packaging expertise. The widget mode should fix the source files.
scenario.switch_pattern: <switch_mode> <mode_slug>baw-coachui-view</mode_slug> <reason>Packaging validation revealed widget structure issues that require implementation fixes.</reason> </switch_mode>

scenario.trigger: User requests widget implementation changes during packaging workflow
scenario.reason: Widget source modifications belong to the widget authoring mode, even if discovered during packaging.

example_phrases.phrase: "Add a new event handler to the widget"

scenario.trigger: User wants to create a new widget before packaging
scenario.reason: New widget creation is widget authoring work. Package after creation is complete.

example_phrases.phrase: "Create a new widget and then package it"

scenario.reason: Widget documentation (README, datamodel.md, eventHandler.md) is maintained by the widget authoring mode.

examples.example: Using MCP tools to deploy to BAW

practice.name: Report widget issues clearly before switching
practice.description: When packaging reveals widget problems, provide a clear summary of what's wrong and which widgets are affected before switching modes.
practice.good_example: "Packaging failed: Stepper widget missing config.json. Switching to widget mode to fix structure."

practice.name: Don't attempt widget fixes in packaging mode
practice.description: Even for "simple" widget issues, switch to the widget mode rather than making quick fixes. This maintains clear responsibility boundaries.

practice.name: Preserve packaging context when switching
practice.description: When switching to widget mode, explain what packaging operation was being attempted so the widget mode understands the full context.
practice.good_example: "Attempting to package Stepper and DateOutput widgets. Stepper has invalid metadata. Switching to fix."

practice.name: Return to packaging after widget fixes
practice.description: After widget mode completes fixes, the user may need to explicitly return to packaging mode or the Orchestrator may route back automatically.

decision.question: Is the issue in widget source files?
Is the issue in packaging configuration or scripts?
Does the user want to modify widget implementation?
Is this a packaging, validation, or deployment task?
decision.no: Consider if another mode is more appropriate

question.q: Should I fix minor widget issues to unblock packaging?
question.a: No. Always switch to widget mode for widget fixes, even if they seem minor. This maintains clear boundaries and prevents scope creep.

question.q: Can I validate widget structure in packaging mode?
question.a: Yes, validation is part of packaging workflow. But if validation reveals issues, switch to widget mode for fixes rather than fixing them yourself.

question.q: What if packaging succeeds but the user wants widget changes?
question.a: Switch to widget mode for the changes, then return to packaging mode to regenerate the TWX with the updated widgets.

question.q: Should I update widget documentation during packaging?
question.a: No. Documentation updates are widget authoring work. Switch to widget mode if documentation needs updating.

anti_pattern.name: Quick-fixing widget files to unblock packaging
anti_pattern.why_bad: Blurs mode responsibilities and can lead to inconsistent widget state
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Switch to widget mode, fix properly, then return to packaging

anti_pattern.name: Assuming packaging failures are always packaging issues
anti_pattern.why_bad: Many packaging failures are actually widget structure problems
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Analyze the error, identify if it's widget or packaging related, then switch modes if needed

anti_pattern.name: Staying in packaging mode for "just one more widget change"
anti_pattern.why_bad: Incremental scope creep leads to the wrong mode handling implementation
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Switch to widget mode as soon as widget changes are requested
